semanticCore: Bee, gift, wedding dowry, thinning, or thin shape
quranicRelevance: Honey bees (Nahl - 'ila an-nahli'), and giving wedding dowries (Nihlah - 'nihlatan').

keyDerivations.meaning: Honey bees, hive insects, or honey producers
Free gift, wedding dowry given willingly, or donation

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَأَوْحَىٰ رَبُّكَ إِلَى النَّحْلِ أَنِ اتَّخِذِي مِنَ الْجِبَالِ بُيُوتًا (16:68): And your Lord inspired/revealed to the bee: 'Take for homes among the mountains' (instinctual biology).
discardedNoise: Physical weight loss and thinning of body (Nuhool) - health logs.
Attributing a poem falsely to an author (Nahl).
summaryEn: The root connects instinctive architecture (bees) with marital gifts, detailing the divine inspiration of honey bees (Nahl) and ordering wedding dowries (Nihlah) given as free gifts.
summaryAr: يربط هذا الجذر بين حشرة النحل صانعة العسل (نحل)، ونحلة الصداق والمهور الطوعية للنساء (نحلة)، واصفاً إلهام النحل الجبلي، ومهر الزواج.
rawText: نَحَِلَ جِسْمُهُ 1 His body became lean, or emaciated. (S.) نِحْلَةٌ i. q. فَرِيضَةٌ; or دِيَانَةٌ; and دِينٌ, as in the saying مَا نِحْلَتُكَ [What is thy religion?]. (TA.)
